DESCRIPTOR.LABEL: ENA Ancient DNA
DESCRIPTOR.NAME: ena_ancient_dna
DESCRIPTOR.DESCRIPTION: This is the ancient DNA checklist compiled at ENA. It is based on the various Genomic Standards Consortium(GSC) packages for reporting of measurements and observations obtained from the environment where the sample was obtained, with many additional fields from the proposed MinAS, and a few more.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: non-sample terms: study or project

FIELD.LABEL: project name
FIELD.NAME: project_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the project within which the sequencing was organized

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: methods, storage and transport

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection device
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_device
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The device used to collect an environmental sample. It is recommended to use terms listed under environmental sampling device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO) and/or terms listed under specimen collection device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0002094).

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection method
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The method employed for collecting the sample. Can be provided in the form of a PMID, DOI, url or text.

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage location
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_location
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Location at which sample was stored, usually name of a specific freezer/room. Indicate the location name.

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage device
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_device
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The container used to store the sample. This field accepts terms listed under container (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C43186). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the storage device.

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage conditions
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_conditions
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Conditions at which sample was stored, usually storage temperature, duration and location. In soil context: Explain how and for how long the soil sample was stored before DNA extraction (fresh/frozen/other).

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Collection event information

FIELD.LABEL: collection date
FIELD.NAME: collection_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The date the sample was collected with the intention of sequencing, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[12][0-9]{3}(-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(-(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?(/[0-9]{4}(-[0-9]{2}(-[0-9]{2}(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?)?$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (region and locality)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_region_and_locality
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the specific region name followed by the locality name.

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (latitude)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_latitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (longitude)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_longitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: broad-scale environmental context
FIELD.NAME: broadscale_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen came from. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvO’s biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD.LABEL: local environmental context
FIELD.NAME: local_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen’s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for "broad-scale environmental context". Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD.LABEL: environmental medium
FIELD.NAME: environmental_medium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top).

FIELD.LABEL: elevation
FIELD.NAME: elevation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The elevation of the sampling site as measured by the vertical distance from mean sea level.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ([+-]?(0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD.LABEL: collected_by
FIELD.NAME: collected_by
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: name of persons or institute who collected the specimen

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Organism characteristics

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (country and/or sea)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_country_andor_sea
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of where the sample was collected from, with the intention of sequencing, as defined by the country or sea name. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).

TEXT_VALUE.VALUE: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Environmental information

FIELD.LABEL: depth
FIELD.NAME: depth
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The vertical distance below local surface, e.g. for sediment or soil samples depth is measured from sediment or soil surface, respectively. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ((0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: site related

FIELD.LABEL: depth inference method
FIELD.NAME: depth_inference_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The method used to estimate the distance from the surface of the sample

FIELD.LABEL: sample coordinator affiliation
FIELD.NAME: sample_coordinator_affiliation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The university, institution, or society affiliation of the sample coordinator.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: local environment conditions

FIELD.LABEL: total depth of water column
FIELD.NAME: total_depth_of_water_column
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: measurement of total depth of water column

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Pointer to physical material

FIELD.LABEL: source material identifiers
FIELD.NAME: source_material_identifiers
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique identifier assigned to a material sample (as defined by http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/materialSampleID, and as opposed to a particular digital record of a material sample) used for extracting nucleic acids, and subsequent sequencing. The identifier can refer either to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples. The INSDC qualifiers /specimen_voucher, /bio_material, or /culture_collection may or may not share the same value as the source_mat_id field. For instance, the /specimen_voucher qualifier and source_mat_id may both contain 'UAM:Herps:14' , referring to both the specimen voucher and sampled tissue with the same identifier. However, the /culture_collection qualifier may refer to a value from an initial culture (e.g. ATCC:11775) while source_mat_id would refer to an identifier from some derived culture from which the nucleic acids were extracted (e.g. xatc123 or ark:/2154/R2).

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: experimental factor and block

FIELD.LABEL: negative control status
FIELD.NAME: negative_control_status
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Is this sample a negative control?

FIELD.LABEL: negative control of
FIELD.NAME: negative_control_of
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to the sample(s) for which the negative control was used. The referenced samples should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx OR a range e.g. ERSxxxxxx-ERSxxxxxx

FIELD.LABEL: negative control type
FIELD.NAME: negative_control_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The substance or equipment used as a negative control in an investigation

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample processing

FIELD.LABEL: sample volume or weight for DNA extraction
FIELD.NAME: sample_volume_or_weight_for_dna_extraction
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Volume (ml) or mass (g) of total collected sample processed for DNA extraction. Note: total sample collected should be entered under the term 'sample size'.

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid extraction
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_extraction
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the material separation to recover the nucleic acid fraction from a sample

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid extraction kit
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_extraction_kit
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The name of the extraction kit used to recover the nucleic acid fraction of an input material is performed.

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid extraction date
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_extraction_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The date when the nucleic acid extraction was started from the sample material. In case no exact time is available, the date can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008; Except: 2008-01; 2008 all are ISO8601 compliant

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: ancient DNA specific

FIELD.LABEL: damage treatment
FIELD.NAME: damage_treatment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Indication of whether characteristic ancient DNA damage has been altered or removed from a DNA extract in a laboratory

FIELD.LABEL: master core sample id
FIELD.NAME: master_core_sample_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A Master ID is the ID of an arbitrary sized set of field samples that acts as a single continuous sample (e.g. a set of correlated cores).

FIELD.LABEL: sample age range youngest limit
FIELD.NAME: sample_age_range_youngest_limit
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The youngest possible age of the sample. Youngest refers to the time closest to present day. If a sample has only a single age value, the value from samp age range oldest limit can be repeated Sample age refers to a period of time in the past, such as when an organism was living and then died, not the biological age.

FIELD.LABEL: sample age range oldest limit
FIELD.NAME: sample_age_range_oldest_limit
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The oldest possible age of the sample. Oldest refers to the time furthest from present day. If a sample has only a single age value, the value from samp age range youngest limit can be repeated. Sample age refers to a period of time in the past, such as when an organism was living and then died, not the biological age.

FIELD.LABEL: sample age inference method
FIELD.NAME: sample_age_inference_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The method used to infer the sample age. This corresponds to a fixed list of most commonly used dating methods, such as 14C, OSL, stratigraphic inference, historical records, other etc. If the method is not in the list, please provide a description in samp_age_range_inference_description.

FIELD.LABEL: sample age inference description
FIELD.NAME: sample_age_inference_description
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Additional general information about the inference method used to infer the sample age that can be useful for understanding how the sample age was estimated. This can be things such as whether radiocarbon age estimated on AMS or conventional radiocarbon dating, or which calibration confidence interval was used. It can also be used to describe what artefacts were used to age the stratigraphic layer of historical contexts.

FIELD.LABEL: host body preservation state
FIELD.NAME: host_body_preservation_state
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of the state of the sampled (ancient) organism/host as originally preserved in the burial environment, e.g. if the the sample was artificially or naturally (fully/partially) mummified at the time of death.

FIELD.LABEL: cultural era
FIELD.NAME: cultural_era
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The cultural era approximating the period in which the individual lived from https://chronontology.dainst.org/ or PeriodO if possible. If not available, follow INSDC standard for reporting missing data (https://www.insdc.org/submitting-standards/missing-value-reporting/).

FIELD.LABEL: palaeopathology status
FIELD.NAME: palaeopathology_status
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Describe briefly any relevant palaeopathological or health-related observations of the remains of the individual under study.

FIELD.LABEL: sample decontamination pretreatment
FIELD.NAME: sample_decontamination_pretreatment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Method(s) employed for surface decontamination of samples of external modern DNA; Treatment used on the samples. Depends on the sample type. More relevant for bones than environmental samples. E.g. buffers, EDTA, etc.

FIELD.LABEL: geological epoch
FIELD.NAME: geological_epoch
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Geological age of source sediment (Additional info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Period_(geology)).

FIELD.LABEL: past broad-scale environmental context
FIELD.NAME: past_broadscale_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen likely from when originally laid down. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvO’s biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD.LABEL: recovery date
FIELD.NAME: recovery_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Date of excavation or retrieval from burial or depositional context, if known. In case no exact time is available, the date can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008; Except: 2008-01; 2008 all are ISO8601 compliant. If excavations were done during a longer period, report its midpoint at a month or year level.

FIELD.LABEL: preservational treatment
FIELD.NAME: preservational_treatment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of any treatment applied to samples for the purpose of maximising collection preservation that may influence downstream DNA recovery or library construction, such as storage fluid or reconstructive glue

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: General collection event information

FIELD.LABEL: sample composed of
FIELD.NAME: sample_composed_of
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to sample(s) that are part of a sample, for example when samples are pooled for analysis or when a group of samples corresponds to a higher level sample. The referenced sample(s) should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx

FIELD.LABEL: collecting institution
FIELD.NAME: collecting_institution
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the institution to which the person collecting the specimen belongs. Format: Institute Name, Institute Address

FIELD.LABEL: past local environmental context
FIELD.NAME: past_local_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen’s local vicinity, likely from when originally laid down and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for "broad-scale environmental context". Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: core sample properties

FIELD.LABEL: sample same as
FIELD.NAME: sample_same_as
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to sample(s) that are equivalent. The referenced sample(s) should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx

FIELD.LABEL: sample derived from
FIELD.NAME: sample_derived_from
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to parental sample(s) or original run(s) that the assembly is derived from. The referenced samples or runs should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample/run e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx OR a range e.g. ERSxxxxxx-ERSxxxxxx

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection

FIELD.LABEL: sample coordinator
FIELD.NAME: sample_coordinator
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The name of the sample coordinator.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: other

FIELD.LABEL: sample material type
FIELD.NAME: sample_material_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The type of material from which the sample was obtained. For the Hydrocarbon package, samples include types like core, rock trimmings, drill cuttings, piping section, coupon, pigging debris, solid deposit, produced fluid, produced water, injected water, swabs, etc. For the Food Package, samples are usually categorized as food, body products or tissues, or environmental material. This field accepts terms listed under environmental specimen (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0001246).
